content:
Hi folks!

Which LSP client do you use for neovim, please?
Or are you using VSCode?

Leandro Coutinho said:

Hi folks!

Which LSP client do you use for neovim, please?
Or are you using VSCode?

maybe this might help: https://rust-analyzer.github.io/manual.html#vimneovim
coc.vim works well enough for my Rust related projects. I have not had a chance to write code for Rust for Linux though.
This should work, have a look at the rustanalyzer make target, which generates the rust-project.json spec

Wei Liu said:

coc.vim works well enough for my Rust related projects. I have not had a chance to write code for Rust for Linux though.

I also use this after switching from YouCompleteMe. it has no idea what's going on for Rust for Linux unfortunately by default. :/

John Baublitz said:

Wei Liu said:

coc.vim works well enough for my Rust related projects. I have not had a chance to write code for Rust for Linux though.

I also use this after switching from YouCompleteMe. it has no idea what's going on for Rust for Linux unfortunately by default. :/

Probably needs a bit more configuration then.
yes most likely. I haven't had the time to set it up yet though.
Leandro Coutinho has marked this topic as resolved.
